question.text: The coupon rate of Floating Rate Bonds (FRBs) issued by the Government of India is primarily linked to which benchmark?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FRB coupons are majorly linked to the 6-month rate, i.e., the 182-day T-Bill rate. The coupon is reset semi-annually based on the average of implicit cut-off yields of the last three 182-day T-Bill auctions, with a fixed spread added. **The Trap:** RBI Repo rate influences money market rates but is not the direct benchmark for FRB coupons. **Cross-Exam Context:** FRB benchmarking is tested in X-A and IV (Interest Rate Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FRBs protect against rising interest rates since coupons reset higher — recommend when expecting a rate-hiking cycle, unlike fixed-rate bonds which lose value when rates rise.
